<title level="1" right="Curse 8" pfs="">[Arrow Attracting](/Curses.aspx?ID=1)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 92

**Usage** curses armor or shield

</column>

An item affected by an _arrow attracting_ curse protects you normally, but it draws ranged attacks like a magnet. Whenever a creature within 120 feet misses with a ranged attack, it must immediately reroll the attack against your AC, affecting you depending on the result of the new attack roll. The _arrow attraction_ curse activates only if you could have been a legitimate target. Creatures that intentionally attempt to miss a ranged attack do not activate an _arrow attraction_ curse. Once the curse has activated for the first time, the item fuses to you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Arrow Attracting

<title level="1" right="Curse 7" pfs="">[Arsonous](/Curses.aspx?ID=2)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 92

**Usage** curses a ring, staff, or wand

</column>

An _arsonous_ curse creates flaws in the mystic pathways that channel magic through an item, allowing excess power to escape as sparks. Whenever you activate the magic item, a random ally within 30 feet takes 1d10 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). If no ally is in range, you take the damage instead. At the GM’s discretion, this curse might ignite an unattended object or the surrounding environment instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Arsonous

<title level="1" right="Curse 6" pfs="">[Bloodbiter](/Curses.aspx?ID=4)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 92

**Usage** curses a piercing or slashing weapon

</column>

A _bloodbiter_ weapon is awakened by violence and fueled by blood. When you make a successful attack with the weapon, it inflicts a wound that deals 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) (in addition to its normal damage), but it also deals 1d6 persistent bleed damage to you. The curse remains dormant until the weapon hits a creature, at which point black thorns protrude from the weapon and dig into your body; the weapon fuses to you and you can’t use the hand that holds the weapon for any other purpose. If the weapon is two-handed, it attaches itself to only a single hand (GM’s choice).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bloodbiter

<title level="1" right="Curse 9" pfs="">[Dependent](/Curses.aspx?ID=6)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 93

**Usage** curses gear used for skills or a weapon

</column>

The _dependent_ curse makes an item function properly only under certain circumstances. Whenever you use the item to perform a skill check or use the weapon in combat, your degree of success is one worse than the result you rolled unless those specific conditions are met. The most common types of _dependent_ curses are nocturnal or diurnal—functioning normally only at night or only during the day, respectively—but more restrictive curses do exist, such as a curse that restricts the item’s use to underground or a curse that allows the item to function effectively only during autumn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dependent

</column>

When you invest this armor, a personal-sized cloud appears over your head and begins to rain on you, and the armor fuses to you. This extinguishes uncovered flames and soaks other objects you are carrying or holding, potentially ruining them. [Cold conditions](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) are one step worse under the cloud, and at the GM’s discretion it might cause other problems, such as interfering with sleep.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title level="1" right="Curse 1" pfs="">[Staining](/Curses.aspx?ID=13)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 93

**Usage** curses gear or a weapon

</column>

This property is associated with a specific color, which is defined at the time of the curse’s creation. Whenever you use the affected equipment while taking a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) action with another object, the second object is permanently stained the associated color. For instance, if you used a set of yellow _staining lockpicks_ to open a door, the lock would permanently become yellow. This color change does not otherwise unnaturally persist and can be changed via any normal mundane or magical means.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Staining

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />

A _withering_ curse shrivels vulnerable flesh. Whenever you activate the item, one of your fingers turns black and falls off. You take a –1 status penalty to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks and Dexterity-based attack rolls with a hand missing two or three fingers; if you lose more than four fingers on one hand, you can’t use that hand to hold objects or use [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) actions. These fingers can be replaced by magic but are otherwise gone forever. The GM has the final say on how creatures with unusual appendages or numbers of fingers are affected by this curse.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Withering

This punishment causes you to lose something dear to you whenever you rob or steal. If you have nothing to lose, the curse exacts its punishment upon your body instead.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 39 Fortitude

**Effect**
Each time you steal something, you lose something more valuable—this item is whisked away and can’t be found again. Typically this item is one of greater monetary value, but it might be one of greater value in another sense, such as one of greater emotional value, or something you need to complete a task. If you aren’t carrying anything of greater value at the time of the theft, you lose one of your limbs instead, taking 10d6 slashing damage and losing use of that limb. The damage can’t be healed, nor the limb restored, until the stolen item is returned, even through use of spells like [_regenerate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=248).

</column>
name: Thief's Retribution

<column gap="tiny">

A grave curse punishes you for stealing from a tomb or other resting place.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
Will save, with a high spell DC for a monster of its level (page 65)

**Effect**
You are hounded by undead creatures of the same level as the curse. Every night, you must attempt a DC 15 flat check. On a failure, an incorporeal undead manifests to hassle and harry you throughout the night, retreating before it can take much damage and often preventing you from gaining a full night’s rest. Whenever you enter a graveyard or other area where bodies are buried, you must succeed at the same flat check or a body animates as a corporeal undead (typically a skeleton or zombie) to attack you.

 These undead are temporary and exist only to harry you; if you take control of the undead, move on, or otherwise avoid their attacks, incorporeal undead discorporate and corporeal undead collapse into ordinary corpses. The curse can be removed by returning the stolen items to their resting place.

</column>
name: Grave Curse

<title level="1" right="Curse 1" pfs="">[Thief's Burden](/Curses.aspx?ID=32)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 162

 

</column>

This ward placed on valuables aims to bog down thieves and prevent them from fleeing.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 15 Fortitude

**Effect**
This curse causes stolen items to weigh you down. While the curse is in effect, you treat any stolen items you carry as 1 Bulk heavier. This increase in Bulk remains in effect until the curse is lifted or the item is returned to its owner.

**Tempted Curse**
Your pockets weigh you down as you dream of filling them with riches. You are encumbered, but you double your total maximum carrying capacity.

</column>
name: Thief's Burden

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 162

 

</column>

Often used to protect ancient tomes, this curse causes your skin to shed like weathered paper, cracking and bleeding at the slightest touch.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 20 Fortitude

**Effect**
Seeping wounds cover your body as this curse makes your skin brittle. Any time you would gain the [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition, increase the value by 1. Any time you take bleed damage, increase the amount of damage by 1.

**Tempted Curse**
Your skin falls from your body and exposes your raw flesh beneath, but the curse holds your flesh together as its magic increases. You gain resistance to [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to half your level and are [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9).

</column>
name: Unraveling Skin

<title level="1" right="Curse 7" pfs="">[Boastful Prince's Scourge](/Curses.aspx?ID=34)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 162

 

</column>

Used as a punishment against the vain or the arrogant, this curse slowly transforms you into a frog in not just body, but mind as well.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 23 Will

**Effect**
Your features slowly morph into that of a frog's as you grow a prominent vocal sac. You gain the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=207) trait and can breathe both air and water. You are [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) as long as you are under the effect of this curse, and you can't reduce your stupefied value below 1.

**Tempted Curse**
You transform fully, as [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10), giving you the specific abilities of a frog. The duration of this casting is until you make your daily preparations, and you can't Dismiss the spell. While you are under the effect of _animal form_ due to this curse, you have all the usual limitations of _animal form_, including the fact that you can't speak (though you can still understand any languages you know).

</column>
name: Boastful Prince's Scourge

<title level="1" right="Curse 7" pfs="">[Sinful Tongue](/Curses.aspx?ID=35)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 162

 

</column>

This curse is typically used as punishment by good clergy. The curse causes you to envision your wrongdoings whenever you attempt to lie or deceive, a burden that can be cured only by confessing your past transgressions.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 23 Will

**Effect**
([misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110)) The weight of guilt distracts you, making it hard for you to deceive people. Whenever you attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check, roll the check twice and take the worse result.

**Tempted Curse**
You begin to confess your sins, beginning from youthful indiscretions and ending with the present day. While you confess, all creatures within 30 feet must succeed at a DC 23 Will save or gain the [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) condition as they listen. You must spend at least 2 actions per round, which have the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, listing your wrongdoings. This is an [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect. Once you stop confessing your sins, or after 10 minutes if you haven't finished confessing your sins, the effect ends, and you can't confess again until your next daily preparations. Your conscience is clearer having confessed, and you take only a –2 status penalty to Deception checks.

</column>
name: Sinful Tongue

<title level="1" right="Curse 9" pfs="">[Swarming Words](/Curses.aspx?ID=36)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 162

 

</column>

Placed upon those who use magic to cause harm, this curse causes a shower of bugs to fall from your lips whenever you speak, foiling your spellcasting.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 26 Fortitude

**Effect**
The crawling within your mouth never stops as cockroaches, ants, and other insects nest in your body. Whenever you attempt to Cast a Spell with a verbal component or otherwise use an activity that has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait, you must attempt a DC 5 flat check. On a failure, the insects [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) your activity as they crowd your mouth and muffle the sound. 

**Tempted Curse**
The insects multiply into a cascade. When you activate the tempted curse, you must spend 2 actions to cast a 5th-level [_vomit swarm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=726) to free the vermin inside you. For 1 minute after unleashing the swarm, you are not affected by the curse, but afterward, the swarming worsens. For the rest of the day, whenever you attempt to Cast a Spell with a verbal component or otherwise use an activity that has the auditory trait, you must attempt a DC 10 flat check. On a failure, the insects counteract your activity.

</column>
name: Swarming Words

<title level="1" right="Curse 10" pfs="">[Rage of the Excommunicant](/Curses.aspx?ID=37)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 163

 

</column>

Placed upon heretics or those who broke faith, this curse causes symbols of your former faith to burn you upon sight.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 27 Will

**Effect**
This curse is always associated with a specific deity, and is typically experienced only by one of their former [clerics](/Classes.aspx?ID=5) or [champions](/Classes.aspx?ID=4). The sight of that deity's [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) burns you, dealing 1d6 damage every round you look upon it. You can avert your eyes to avoid losing Hit Points once you know where the symbol is located. In addition, the curse hardens you against that god. You gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws and AC versus [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) spells cast by that deity's clergy.

**Tempted Curse**
The sight of the deity's religious symbol burns you with the god's rage. You can activate the tempted curse upon viewing the deity's symbol. You are [quickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 minute and can use the extra action each round only to Strike and Stride. For the rest of the day, whenever you see the deity's religious symbol, you lose 2d6 Hit Points.

</column>
name: Rage of the Excommunicant

<title level="1" right="Curse 11" pfs="">[Forbidding Knowledge](/Curses.aspx?ID=38)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 163

 

</column>

Information you never should have known lingers everpresent in your mind.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 28 Will

**Effect**
This curse is acquired when encountering forbidden knowledge. This curse is always associated with a specific piece or topic of knowledge, such as a demon lord's plan to overtake a government or the true name of a powerful wizard. Your tongue trips when you try to tell another this knowledge, and your hand stalls when you attempt to write it. Any attempt at communicating it fails, even as you can't stop thinking about it.

**Tempted Curse**
When you activate the tempted curse, you must spend 2 actions to attempt to speak the triggering knowledge. This allows you to redirect the curse's energy and cast [_silence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=287) heightened to 4th level, centered on yourself. You are still unable to speak the knowledge clearly, but you can convey a single word to an adjacent creature. You gain the [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) condition for the remainder of the day. This condition can't be reduced until the next time you perform your daily preparations, at which point it ends.

</column>
name: Forbidding Knowledge

<title level="1" right="Curse 13" pfs="">[Enforced Hospitality](/Curses.aspx?ID=39)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 163

 

</column>

Cast upon the rude or inconsiderate, this curse compels you to offer a genuine and warm greeting to each new creature you meet.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 31 Will

**Effect**
This curse compels you to great those you meet as though they were old friends. Whenever you come across a creature (or group of creatures) for the first time, you must spend 1 action greeting them or become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 minute, even if the creature is obviously [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=23). No matter your intention, your greeting comes across as warm and genuine, and it grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50).

**Tempted Curse**
Your compulsion to greet newcomers intensifies. You must greet every individual creature you meet for the first time by spending 1 action greeting them or become slowed 1 for 1 minute. If you meet more creatures than you have actions, you can continue spending actions greeting creatures across multiple turns. For example, if you encountered six bandits, you could greet three bandits on the first round and three bandits on the second round, and you wouldn't become slowed unless you took another action before you finished greeting them. For 1 minute or until you take a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), you gain the effects of a 7th-level [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) against creatures you've greeted in this way (DC 31). 

</column>
name: Enforced Hospitality

<title level="1" right="Curse 6" pfs="">[Droskar's Gloom](/Curses.aspx?ID=40)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Crown of the Kobold King](/Sources.aspx?ID=148) pg. 96

 

</column>

You grow increasingly glum and stoic, viewing frivolity with disdain and eventually forgetting how to smile or take joy in anything. Over time, you become obsessive about work, toiling away at menial tasks (especially crafting, masonry, or smithing).

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 22 Will

**Effect**
Whenever you attempt to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23) or a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check for any reason, you become obsessed with perfection to the point you hamper your chances of success. Roll the Earn Income or Crafting check twice and take the worst result. If you ever critically fail at a Crafting check or a check to Earn Income, you become [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15). In addition, you suffer a –1 status penalty on all Charisma-based checks as your personality suffers under the effects of this curse. A [dwarf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) who dies while under the effects of Droskar's gloom must attempt a DC 5 flat check; on a failure, the dwarf rises as a [forge-spurned](/Monsters.aspx?ID=473) 1d4 rounds after death occurs.

</column>

<title level="1" right="Curse 7" pfs="">[Lamashtu's Bloom](/Curses.aspx?ID=41)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=46" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 242

 

</column>

While some NPCs become afflicted by Lamashtu’s bloom curse during Parts 1 and 2 of this chapter, during Part 3 the PCs may be exposed to it as well. While the bloom is itself a curse, it functions mechanically more like a disease, and as such features both of these affliction traits. As long as the curse persists, a creature cannot recover from the disease, and removing the curse does not automatically remove the disease element of the affliction. Both elements must be dealt with individually before a victim suffering from Lamashtu’s Bloom is safe. As with all elements of this curse, slaying the bloom of Lamashtu in area C10 automatically removes this affliction.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 23 Fort

**Effect**
**Onset** 1 week; **Stage 1** no ill effects (1 day); **Stage 2** enfeebled 1 (1 day); **Stage 3** enfeebled 1 and 2d6 poison damage (8 hours); **Stage 4** unconscious and 4d6 poison damage (10 minutes); **Stage 5** death; **Special** a creature that dies from Lamashtu's bloom explodes in a blast of First World foliage, releasing a monster. This creature is typically a low-intelligence beast or dragon between 7th and 9th level, depending on the GM's whim.

</column>
name: Lamashtu's Bloom

<title level="1" right="Curse 10" pfs="">[Cleansing Curse](/Curses.aspx?ID=42)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 73

 

</column>

Your body and mind weaken as you’re increasingly afflicted by the ravages of time, aging at an accelerated pace.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 27 Fortitude

**Effect**
You become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37), each with a value of 1. If you critically fail this saving throw, the value of one of these conditions, chosen at random, is instead 2. If you already have any of these conditions, its value instead increases by the given amount. In time, the cleansing curse will cause your death from old age, but this eventuality takes weeks, or even a month or two—if the PCs haven't defeated the Kingdom of the Cleansed and used the magical pool in area D7 to remove this affliction by the time two months have passed, the GM should decide if some or all of them perish (perhaps by having them attempt daily DC 27 Fortitude saving throws to avoid death after a period of 2 months have passed).

</column>
name: Cleansing Curse

<title level="1" right="Curse 5" pfs="">[Curse of Biting Swarms](/Curses.aspx?ID=44)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 176

 

</column>

You emit a deathlike stench that attracts swarms of carrion-eating insects.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 20 Fort

**Effect**
You smell like a charnel house, making it easy for creatures with [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) to notice you; they gain a +2 circumstance bonus to their Perception against you. Additionally, the first time you're exposed to sunlight each day, biting insects attack you, scattering once they've taken damage. You then become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) until the next time you take a full night's rest. These conditions can't be reduced below 1 by other means.

</column>
name: Curse of Biting Swarms

<title level="1" right="Curse 9" pfs="">[Curse of the Rotting Heart](/Curses.aspx?ID=45)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 176

 

</column>

A shriveled, spectral heart appears inside your body, beating alongside your own.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 25 Will

**Effect**
You're harmed by positive energy as if you were [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), harmed by negative energy as if you were living, and can't be healed by either one. All other forms of magical healing, such as the [_soothe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=291) spell, restore only half the normal amount of Hit Points. Alchemical healing, healing with the [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) skill, and other non-magical methods continue to heal you normally.

</column>
name: Curse of the Rotting Heart

<title level="1" right="Curse 20" pfs="">[Boatman's Toll](/Curses.aspx?ID=46)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Death" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=40" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 176

 

</column>

Your soul is treated as collateral for the crimes you've committed. This punishment is reserved for those who have committed truly heinous acts, such as using holy magic against an important [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) citizen or conspiring with enemies of the state.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 43 Will

**Effect**
A tattoo of a skull with coins on its eyes etches itself into your skin, dealing 6d10 cold damage and 6d10 negative damage and causing you to become [drained 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). If the tattoo is ever removed or destroyed by any means, it reappears after 24 hours and deals its damage again unless this curse is removed first. If you're reduced to 0 Hit Points while the tattoo is on your body, you die immediately, and your soul is banished to [Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=12). You can't be revived, reincarnated, raised as an undead, or otherwise come back to life unless the boatman [Charon](/Deities.aspx?ID=135) relinquishes your soul.

</column>
name: Boatman's Toll

DC 23 Will

**Effect**

DC 31 Will

**Effect**

<title level="1" right="Curse 10" pfs="">[Obnubilate Curse](/Curses.aspx?ID=49)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />

**Source** [Pathfinder #187: The Seventh Arch](/Sources.aspx?ID=192) pg. 79

 

</column>

Only [elves](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) can be afflicted with this magical curse; other creatures are immune. An elf who touches the [_Shadewither Key_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1818) must save against the curse. Victims quickly fall [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38); then, over several days, they slowly blacken and shrivel like rotting fruit until they die. The corpse of an elf killed by the _obnubilate curse_ is dry and charcoal-like; it collapses into ash at the slightest touch.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 27 Fortitude

**Effect**
**Onset** 1 minute; **Stage 1** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) and 3d6 mental damage (1 day); **Stage 2** unconscious and 6d6 mental damage (2 days); **Stage 3** unconscious and 6d6 mental damage (2 days); **Stage 4** unconscious and 6d6 mental damage, permanent loss of most recent year of memory (1 day); **Stage 5** unconscious and 6d6 mental damage, permanent loss of all memory (1 day); **Stage 6** death.

</column>
name: Obnubilate Curse

**Saving Throw**
DC 26 Will

This property is associated with a specific color, which is defined at the time of the curse's creation. Whenever you use the affected equipment while taking a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) action with another object, the second object is permanently stained the associated color. For instance, if you used a set of yellow staining lockpicks to open a door, the lock would permanently become yellow. This color change does not otherwise unnaturally persist and can be changed via any normal mundane or magical means.

**Saving Throw**
DC 39 Fortitude

Each time you steal something, you lose something more valuable—this item is whisked away and can't be found again. The damage can't be healed, nor the limb restored, until the stolen item is returned, even through use of spells like [_regenerate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1648). 

</column>

<title level="1" right="Curse Level Varies" pfs="">[Grave Curse](/Curses.aspx?ID=92)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 87

 

</column>

A grave curse punishes you for stealing from a tomb or other resting place.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
Will save, with a high spell DC for a [monster of its level](/Rules.aspx?ID=2899)

**Effect**
You are hounded by undead creatures of the same level as the curse. Every night, you must attempt a DC 15 flat check. On a failure, an incorporeal undead manifests to hassle and harry you throughout the night, retreating before it can take much damage and often preventing you from gaining a full night's rest. Whenever you enter a graveyard or other area where bodies are buried, you must succeed at the same flat check or a body animates as a corporeal undead (typically a skeleton or zombie) to attack you.

 These undead are temporary and exist only to harry you; if you take control of the undead, move on, or otherwise avoid their attacks, incorporeal undead discorporate and corporeal undead collapse into ordinary corpses. The curse can be removed by returning the stolen items to their resting place.

</column>
name: Grave Curse

Only [elves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=60) can be afflicted with this magical curse; other creatures are immune. An elf who touches the _[Shadewither Key](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1818)_ must save against the curse. Victims quickly fall unconscious; then, over several days, they slowly blacken and shrivel like rotting fruit until they die. The corpse of an elf killed by the _obnubilate curse_ is dry and charcoal-like; it collapses into ash at the slightest touch.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 27 Fortitude

**Effect**
**Onset** 1 minute; **Stage 1** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and 3d6 mental damage (1 day); **Stage 2** unconscious and 6d6 mental damage (2 days); **Stage 3** unconscious and 6d6 mental damage (2 days); **Stage 4** unconscious and 6d6 mental damage, permanent loss of most recent year of memory (1 day); **Stage 5** unconscious and 6d6 mental damage, permanent loss of all memory (1 day); **Stage 6** death.

</column>
name: Obnubilate Curse

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 20 Fortitude

**Effect**
Seeping wounds cover your body as this curse makes your skin brittle. Any time you would gain the [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition, increase the value by 1. Any time you take bleed damage, increase the amount of damage by 1.

**Tempted Curse**
Your skin falls from your body and exposes your raw flesh beneath, but the curse holds your flesh together as its magic increases. You gain resistance [to persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to half your level and are [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1.

</column>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 162

 

</column>

Used as a punishment against the vain or the arrogant, this curse slowly transforms you into a frog in not just body, but mind as well.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 23 Will

**Effect**
Your features slowly morph into that of a frog's as you grow a prominent vocal sac. You gain the amphibious trait and can breathe both air and water. You're [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 as long as you're under the effect of this curse, and you can't reduce your stupefied value below 1.

**Tempted Curse**
You transform fully, as [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1440), giving you the specific abilities of a frog. The duration of this casting is until you make your daily preparations, and you can't Dismiss the spell. While you're under the effect of animal form due to this curse, you have all the usual limitations of animal form, including the fact that you can't speak (though you can still understand any languages you know).

</column>
name: Boastful Prince's Scourge

<title level="1" right="Curse 7" pfs="">[Sinful Tongue](/Curses.aspx?ID=101)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 162

 

</column>

This curse is typically used as punishment by clergy who revere honesty. The curse causes you to envision your wrongdoings whenever you attempt to lie or deceive, a burden that can be cured only by confessing your past transgressions.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 23 Will

**Effect**
The weight of guilt distracts you, making it hard for you to deceive people. Whenever you attempt a Deception check, roll the check twice and take the worse result; this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect.

**Tempted Curse**
You begin to confess your sins, beginning from youthful indiscretions and ending with the present day. While you confess, all creatures within 30 feet must succeed at a DC 23 Will save or gain the [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) condition as they listen. You must spend at least 2 actions per round, which have the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait, listing your wrongdoings. This is an [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect. Once you stop confessing your sins, or after 10 minutes if you haven't finished confessing your sins, the effect ends, and you can't confess again until your next daily preparations. Your conscience is clearer having confessed, and you take only a –2 status penalty to Deception checks.

</column>
name: Sinful Tongue

<traits>

Placed upon those who use magic to cause harm, this curse causes a shower of bugs to fall from your lips whenever you speak, foiling your spellcasting.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 26 Fortitude

**Effect**
The crawling within your mouth never stops as cockroaches, ants, and other insects nest in your body. Whenever you attempt to Cast a Spell requiring incantations (as most do) or use an activity that has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait, you must attempt a DC 5 flat check. On a failure, the insects disrupt your activity as they crowd your mouth and muffle the sound.

**Tempted Curse**
The insects multiply into a cascade. When you activate the tempted curse, you must spend 2 actions to cast a 5th-rank [_vomit swarm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2041) to free the vermin inside you. For 1 minute after unleashing the swarm, you aren't affected by the curse, but afterward, the swarming worsens. For the rest of the day, whenever you attempt to Cast a Spell requiring incantations or use an activity that has the auditory trait, you must attempt a DC 10 flat check. On a failure, the insects disrupt your activity.

</column>

<traits>

Placed upon heretics or those who broke faith, this curse causes symbols of your former faith to burn you upon sight.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 27 Will

**Effect**
 This curse is always associated with a specific deity, and is typically experienced only by one of their former clerics or champions. The sight of that deity's religious symbol burns you, dealing 1d6 spirit damage every round you look upon it. You can avert your eyes to avoid losing Hit Points once you know where the symbol is located. In addition, the curse hardens you against that god. You gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws and AC versus divine spells cast by that deity's clergy.

**Tempted Curse**
The sight of the deity's religious symbol burns you with the god's rage. You can activate the tempted curse upon viewing the deity's symbol. You are [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 minute and can use the extra action each round only to Strike and Stride. For the rest of the day, for each round you look upon the deity's religious symbol, you take 2d6 spirit damage.

</column>

<title level="1" right="Curse 11" pfs="">[Forbidding Knowledge](/Curses.aspx?ID=104)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 163

 

</column>

Information you never should’ve known lingers ever-present in your mind.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 28 Will

**Effect**
This curse is acquired when encountering forbidden knowledge. This curse is always associated with a specific piece or topic of knowledge, such as a demon lord's plan to overtake a government or the true name of a powerful wizard. Your tongue trips when you try to tell another this knowledge, and your hand stalls when you attempt to write it. Any attempt at communicating it fails, even as you can't stop thinking about it.

**Tempted Curse**
When you activate the tempted curse, you must spend 2 actions to attempt to speak the triggering knowledge. This allows you to redirect the curse's energy and cast [_silence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1674) heightened to 4th rank, centered on yourself. You're still unable to speak the knowledge clearly, but you can convey a single word to an adjacent creature. You gain the [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 condition for the remainder of the day. This condition can't be reduced until the next time you perform your daily preparations, at which point it ends.

</column>

<title level="1" right="Curse 13" pfs="">[Enforced Hospitality](/Curses.aspx?ID=105)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 163

 

</column>

Cast upon the rude or inconsiderate, this curse compels you to offer a genuine and warm greeting to each new creature you meet.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 31 Will

**Effect**
This curse compels you to great those you meet as though they were old friends. Whenever you come across a creature (or group of creatures) for the first time, you must spend 1 action greeting them or become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 minute, even if the creature is obviously hostile. No matter your intention, your greeting comes across as warm and genuine, and it grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to Diplomacy checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392)

**Tempted Curse**
Your compulsion to greet newcomers intensifies. You must greet every individual creature you meet for the first time by spending 1 action greeting them or become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 minute. If you meet more creatures than you have actions, you can continue spending actions greeting creatures across multiple turns. For example, if you encountered six bandits, you could greet three bandits on the first round and three bandits on the second round, and you wouldn't become slowed unless you took another action before you finished greeting them. For 1 minute or until you take a hostile action, you gain the effects of a 7th-rank [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660) spell against creatures you've greeted in this way (DC 31).

</column>
name: Enforced Hospitality

<title level="1" right="Curse 5" pfs="">[Face at the Foot of the Bed](/Curses.aspx?ID=110)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=566" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #197: Let the Leaves Fall](/Sources.aspx?ID=222) pg. 27

 

</column>

You’ve come face-to-face with your own ghost, and something is keeping it trapped.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw**
DC 19 Will

**Effect**
You realize that you’ve witnessed your own ghost, peering back from the future in an attempt to warn you of what will soon kill you, only for that same unknown force to tear your ghost away and leave you cursed with the conviction that what will eventually kill you now knows where you are. You suffer a –1 penalty to all saving throws against fear effects. Additionally, each time you’re reduced to 0 Hit Points, you grow that much closer to your true death and become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for the next 24 hours. This duration doesn’t stack but does reset each time you nearly die.

</column>
name: Face at the Foot of the Bed
